title: 'Off With His Head': What Good is Prince Charming?
gist: An essay arguing that modern Disney retellings (Shrek, Tangled, Moana, the 2025 live-action Snow White) systematically cut or diminish the prince figure, swapping female maturation narratives for 'female empowerment,' and that this cultural shift discourages young women from marriage and demoralizes young men.

insights.claim: Fairy tales function as 'tuning forks for civilization' whose symbolic structures cannot be subverted without distorting the broader culture, per symbolism educator Jonathan Pageau.
insights.speaker: Morgan Morin
insights.so_what: Frames children's media as a load-bearing transmitter of marriage/family norms, implying content trends are a leading indicator of future family-formation attitudes.
insights.locator: Fairy tales are not superfluous stories

insights.claim: In traditional fairy tales the prince symbolizes love, marriage, and family, serving as the resolution that moves the female protagonist from adolescent chaos into mature womanhood.
insights.speaker: Morgan Morin
insights.so_what: Establishes the symbolic baseline against which the author measures modern subversion of marriage narratives.
insights.locator: In many of these unforgettable tales, Pageau has written

insights.claim: Disney's Tangled (2010), Moana (2016), and the 2025 live-action Snow White each remove or diminish the prince/love-interest, with Snow White's final scene literally cropping the male lead out of frame to center the heroine.
insights.speaker: Morgan Morin
insights.so_what: A consistent multi-film pattern at the dominant children's-content producer signals durable demand-side messaging that downplays marriage, relevant to long-run family-formation culture.
insights.quote: the camera pans to Snow White’s face, cutting Jonathan’s head out of the frame completely
insights.locator: In the 2025 live action Snow White

insights.claim: Replacing 'female maturation' arcs with 'female empowerment' arcs removes the need for character growth, casts men as optional or obstructive, and devalues marriage and family.
insights.speaker: Morgan Morin
insights.so_what: Connects narrative framing to declining marriage interest and rising male demoralization, a cultural driver behind falling fertility.
insights.quote: When female empowerment replaces female maturation, young women are encouraged to focus mainly on themselves rather than give of themselves.

insights.claim: The author links the cultural erasure of male protagonists to real-world outcomes: young people delaying marriage, fertility rates plummeting across educational lines, and large numbers of young men seeing themselves as failures.
insights.speaker: Morgan Morin
insights.so_what: Ties media critique to measurable demographic trends (fertility decline, male disengagement) that have macro consequences for labor force and consumption.
insights.quote: Young people are delaying marriage,  and fertility rates are plummeting across educational lines. A huge number of young men see themselves as failures.
insights.locator: Whether or not it is intentional
